items.question: Which workflow most likely causes preprocessing leakage?
options: Split, fit scaler on train, transform test
Pipeline with CV on raw features
fit_transform scaler on all X, then split
items.answer: fit_transform scaler on all X, then split
items.explanation: Global fit_transform before splitting lets test rows influence mean and variance.

items.question: A feature records whether a loan was charged off six months after approval. Used to predict default at approval time, this is primarily:
items.explanation: Post-approval outcome information is unavailable at approval; the feature encodes future knowledge.

items.question: Why wrap StandardScaler and LogisticRegression in one Pipeline for cross_val_score?
options: Each fold refits the scaler on train only
items.answer: Each fold refits the scaler on train only
items.explanation: Pipeline ensures preprocessing parameters are learned per fold from training indices only.

items.question: Panel data with many rows per user_id should be split by:
options: Group or time so users do not span train and test
items.answer: Group or time so users do not span train and test
items.explanation: Random row splits leak near-duplicate user history across partitions.

items.question: Which statement about leakage is correct?
options: No leakage if test labels are never passed to fit
Leakage only happens with neural networks
Test rows in scaler fit still cause leakage
items.answer: Test rows in scaler fit still cause leakage
items.explanation: Any learner that uses test distribution in preprocessing contaminates evaluation, even if the estimator never sees test labels.
